column1: This document defines the permanent data structure for the topic database used by the ONNELLAB Content Engine.
The topic database is the central queue for planning
Every article must originate from a topic record.
No article should be generated outside the topic database.
The CSV file is the canonical topic queue for v1.

column3: or another database is allowed only when the CSV structure becomes insufficient.

column1: The topic database manages content intent.
It does not store the full article.
Each row represents one potential or published article.

column3: and generated outputs remain in separate files.

column1: Column order should remain fixed in v1.
* Must not depend on the article title.
* Must be unique across all categories and languages.
Each topic must belong to exactly one primary category.
Cross-domain relationships are handled through tags and internal links
The real user question the article must answer.
How can I read a very large TXT file without lag?
* Must be written as a complete question.
* Must not begin with an ONNELLAB product name.
* Must remain understandable without additional context.
How to Read Large TXT Files Without Lag
The final article title may differ slightly
column2: but the search intent must remain unchanged.

column1: * No dates unless the date is essential.
* No application name unless the article is specifically about that product.
* Must be unique within the same language.
* English and Korean counterparts for the same article should share the same slug.
Future languages may be added later.
Each translated article must use a separate topic row with its own ID.
* high: strong user demand or launch relevance
Each topic must use exactly one primary search intent.
ONNELLAB applications naturally related to the topic.

* Do not include unrelated apps for promotional purposes.
* Must not be repeated unnaturally in the article.

A future `translation_of` field may connect them.
If the same intent already exists:
* Commas inside fields must be quoted.
* Line breaks inside fields are prohibited.
* Column names must not be changed without updating the parser and this specification.
The topic database is invalid if:
* Slug is duplicated within the same language.
* Published status has no published URL.
* Scheduled status has no scheduled datetime.
* Related app names do not match the official app registry.
The publishing pipeline must stop when validation fails.
The v1 topic database must not include:
* every article is traceable to one topic record
* automation can safely select and update topics
* the CSV remains understandable to a human
